description: city in British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: The City of North Vancouver is a municipality city on the North Shore of the Burrard Inlet, in British Columbia, Canada.

description: country in South America and Oceania, with a claim in Antarctica
description_wiki: Chile, officially the Republic of Chile, is a country in western South America.

description: island of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Vancouver Island is an island in the northeastern Pacific Ocean and part of the Canadian province of British Columbia.

description: indigenous people of southwestern British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: The Squamish people (Squamish: Sḵwx̱wú7mesh, Squamish pronunciation: [ˈsqʷχʷuː.ʔməʃ], historically transliterated as Sko-ko-mish) are an Indigenous people of the Pacific Northwest Coast.

surface_forms: right at the footing of the bridge there
description: suspension bridge in Vancouver, Canada
description_wiki: The Lions Gate Bridge, opened in 1938 and officially known as the First Narrows Bridge, is a suspension bridge that crosses the first narrows of Burrard Inlet and connects the City of Vancouver, British Columbia, to the North Shore municipalities of the District of North Vancouver, the City of North Vancouver, and West Vancouver.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Tacoma ( tə-KOH-mə) is a city in and the county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States.

description: river in North America; flows from British Columbia, through Washington, and along the Washington-Oregon border to the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Columbia River (Upper Chinook: Wimahl or Wimal; Sahaptin: Nch’i-Wàna or Nchi wana; Sinixt dialect swah'netk'qhu) is the largest river in the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: nation and ethnic group native to Wales
description_wiki: The Welsh (Welsh: Cymry) are an ethnic group and nation native to Wales who share a common ancestry, history and culture.

description: land in Canada set aside by the Indian Act for First Nations
description_wiki: In Canada, an Indian reserve (French: réserve indienne) or First Nations reserve (French: réserve des premières nations) is defined by the Indian Act as a "tract of land, the legal title to which is vested in His Majesty, that has been set apart by His Majesty for the use and benefit of a band." Reserves are areas set aside for First Nations, one of the major groupings of Indigenous peoples in Canada, after a contract with the Canadian state ("the Crown"), and are not to be confused with Indigenous peoples' claims to ancestral lands under Aboriginal title.

description: painting by Bruno Bobak
shipping Company
description_wiki: General Steamship Company was founded in 1920 in Houston, Texas, United States, as a Private Company, and now goes by Gensteam since 1996.

description: port in Metro Vancouver
description_wiki: The Port of Vancouver is the largest port in Canada and the fourth largest in North America by tonnes of cargo, facilitating trade between Canada and more than 170 world economies.

description: seat of Multnomah County, and largest city in state of Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Portland ( PORT-lənd) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Oregon.

description: city in and county seat of Jefferson County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Port Townsend  is a city on the Quimper Peninsula in Jefferson County, Washington, United States.

description: labor union
description_wiki: The International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) is a labor union which primarily represents dock workers on the West Coast of the United States, Hawaii, and in British Columbia, Canada; on the East Coast, the dominant union is the International Longshoremen's Association.

description: American labor leader (1901–1990)
description_wiki: Harry Bridges (28 July 1901 – 30 March 1990) was an Australian-born American union leader, first with the International Longshoremen's Association (ILA).

description: phenomenon in the United States of making accusations of subversion or treason without proper regard for evidence, named after Senator Joseph McCarthy
description_wiki: McCarthyism is a political practice defined by the political repression and persecution of left-wing individuals and a campaign spreading fear of communist and Soviet influence on American institutions and of Soviet espionage in the United States during the late 1940s through the 1950s, heavily associated with the Second Red Scare, also known as the McCarthy era.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.
